description_long: This barbecue grill features a robust, rectangular body constructed from dark, likely metal material, with a stepped, angular design on the sides and back. The main grilling area is defined by a set of parallel metal grates, beneath which a glowing orange surface suggests the presence of hot coals or embers. The grill stands on four sturdy legs, providing stability and elevating the cooking surface above the ground. The back of the grill rises higher than the front, forming a partial windbreak or heat shield, while the open top allows for easy access to the cooking area. There are no visible handles, wheels, or side shelves, emphasizing a simple, utilitarian design. The overall appearance is functional and industrial, suitable for public parks or communal outdoor spaces.
description: A sturdy, rectangular barbecue grill with a stepped back, metal grates, and glowing coals beneath, supported by four solid legs. The design is simple and industrial, with no visible side shelves or wheels.
description_view_0: Front view shows the solid, stepped back panel and the rectangular body, with the grilling area partially obscured.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the open grilling area with parallel metal grates and glowing coals beneath, as well as the stepped back and sturdy legs.
description_view_2: Top-down view highlights the grilling surface, metal grates, and the recessed area for coals, with the stepped back visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the open grill, grates, and glowing coals, with the stepped back and legs clearly visible.
